skill.name: youtube-video-api-skill
skill.description: Extract channel-level and video detail data from any public YouTube channel using the BrowserAct API. Supports fetching latest, popular, or earliest videos with metrics like views, likes, comments, and subscriber counts.
skill.summary: Extract structured YouTube channel and video data without manual scraping.
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: The skill uses a Python script to call the BrowserAct API for YouTube data extraction. It accesses the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable and makes HTTP requests to api.browseract.com. These patterns are legitimate and expected for this skill's purpose. The static scanner produced 52 findings, of which most (38) are false positives related to markdown formatting in SKILL.md being misinterpreted as shell execution or weak cryptography. The remaining true positive findings confirm expected behavior: network access to a third-party API, environment variable access for API key, and script execution. No evidence of malicious intent, data exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection was found. The skill should be published with a security warning about third-party API usage and credential management.

medium_findings.title: API Key Accessed from Environment Variable and Sent via Network
medium_findings.description: The script reads BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment variables and transmits it as a Bearer token in HTTP Authorization headers. This is legitimate API authentication behavior, but the combination of credential access and network transmission represents a risk if the environment is compromised.

medium_findings.title: Heuristic: Dangerous Combination of Code Execution, Network, and Credential Access
medium_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged the combination of script execution (Python), network requests, and credential access as a heuristic risk. Evaluation confirms these capabilities exist but are used legitimately for calling the documented BrowserAct API. No evidence of data exfiltration, obfuscation, or malicious payloads was found.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded API Endpoint URL
low_findings.description: The BrowserAct API base URL is hardcoded in the script. This is expected for a skill that targets a specific API service, but poses a risk if the third-party service is compromised or changes ownership.

low_findings.title: No Input Validation on YouTube Channel URL
low_findings.description: The YouTube channel URL is passed directly from user input to an API call without validation. While the API server handles invalid URLs gracefully, there is no client-side validation of the URL format before it is sent over the network.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
dangerous_patterns.description: Script accesses BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment and uses it in HTTP Bearer token authentication. Required and expected for this API-based skill.

dangerous_patterns.title: HTTP Requests to External API
dangerous_patterns.description: Script makes HTTP POST and GET requests to a third-party API (api.browseract.com) for task submission, status polling, and result retrieval. This is the core functionality of the skill.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube Video Data with BrowserAct API
content.value_statement: Manually collecting YouTube channel and video data is slow and error-prone. This skill automates extraction of structured video metrics including views, likes, comments, subscriber counts, and posting schedules through a single API request.
actual_capabilities: Extract channel information including subscriber count and channel title from any public YouTube channel
Retrieve video details such as view count, like count, comment count, and video duration
Filter videos by type: latest, most popular, or earliest uploads
Output structured JSON data ready for analysis or database import
Handle long-running tasks with status polling and automatic retry on failure
limitations: Requires a valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Depends on a third-party API service that may have rate limits or downtime
Cannot access private YouTube videos or channels requiring login
Extraction speed depends on the BrowserAct service and can take several minutes for large channels

use_cases.title: Competitor Channel Analysis
use_cases.description: Track a competitor YouTube channel by extracting their latest videos and engagement metrics. Monitor posting frequency, view counts, and subscriber growth over time.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing analysts and competitive intelligence researchers

use_cases.title: Content Creator Portfolio Review
use_cases.description: Analyze a creator's most popular videos to understand what content drives engagement. Compare like-to-view ratios and comment activity across their catalog.
use_cases.target_user: Content strategists and creator economy investors

use_cases.title: Video Performance Reporting
use_cases.description: Automate collection of video performance data for regular reporting. Extract structured metrics from owned channels including earliest and latest uploads for trend analysis.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content operations teams

prompt_templates.title: Extract Latest Videos from a Channel
prompt_templates.prompt: Get the latest 20 videos from the YouTube channel at https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelName. Extract their titles, view counts, like counts, and publish dates.

prompt_templates.title: Find Most Popular Videos
prompt_templates.prompt: Find the most popular videos from https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelName. Show me which videos have the highest view counts and best like-to-view ratios.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze Channel Growth
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare the earliest and latest videos from https://www.youtube.com/@ChannelName. How has their content strategy and engagement changed over time?

prompt_templates.title: Competitor Benchmarking Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract channel info and recent popular videos from https://www.youtube.com/@Competitor1 and https://www.youtube.com/@Competitor2. Compare subscriber counts, average views, posting frequency, and engagement rates side by side.

output_examples.input: Get channel info and latest videos from https://www.youtube.com/@BrowserAct
output: Channel Title: BrowserAct | Subscribers: 12,500 | Channel URL: https://www.youtube.com/@BrowserAct

Latest Videos: 1. 'API Automation Guide' (45,230 views, 1,200 likes, 89 comments) | 2. 'Browser Automation Tips' (32,100 views, 950 likes, 67 comments) | 3. 'Workflow Optimization' (28,700 views, 780 likes, 45 comments)

output_examples.input: Show me the most popular videos from a tech YouTube channel
output: Found 10 videos sorted by popularity. Top 3: 'React Tutorial 2026' (250K views, 8.2K likes), 'TypeScript Basics' (180K views, 5.1K likes), 'Node.js Guide' (120K views, 3.4K likes). All videos retrieved with view counts, like counts, and publish dates.
Channel has 89,000 subscribers. Average video gets 45,000 views.

best_practices: Set the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable before using the skill for the first time. Store the key securely and never commit it to version control.
Use the Popular video type for competitive analysis to see what content performs best on a target channel.
Monitor task status output during execution. The script prints timestamped progress logs - wait for completion before assuming failure.
anti_patterns: Do not run multiple extractions on the same channel simultaneously. This may trigger rate limits on the BrowserAct API.
Do not rely on this skill for real-time data. API responses may have a delay of several minutes depending on queue length.
Do not use the Earliest video type for large channels with thousands of videos. The extraction may time out before reaching the oldest content.

faq.question: What is the BROWSERACT_API_KEY and where do I get one?
faq.answer: It is an API key required to authenticate with the BrowserAct service. You can obtain one by signing up at browseract.com and visiting the integrations page in your console.

faq.question: How long does video extraction usually take?
faq.answer: Extraction time varies from 30 seconds to several minutes depending on channel size and BrowserAct service load. The script prints status updates every 10 seconds during processing.

faq.question: Can this skill extract data from private YouTube channels?
faq.answer: No. The skill only works with public YouTube channels. Private or unlisted videos cannot be accessed through this API.

faq.question: What happens if the API key is invalid or expired?
faq.answer: The script will return an error containing Invalid authorization. You should then obtain a new API key from your BrowserAct console and update the environment variable.

faq.question: Does this skill work with any YouTube channel URL format?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports standard YouTube channel URLs including the /@Handle format and /channel/ID format. The API resolves the channel internally.

faq.question: What video data fields are included in the output?
faq.answer: Channel-level data includes channel title, URL, and subscriber count. Per-video data includes title, URL, publish date, view count, duration, comment count, and like count where available.
